popularStory.headline: Wikipedia
popularStory.description: Henry Bourchier, 5th Baron Bourchier, 2nd Count of Eu, 1st Viscount Bourchier, 1st Earl of Essex, KG (c. 1404-1406 – 4 April 1483), was the eldest son of William Bourchier, 1st Count of Eu and Anne of …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex於大約1405年出生在英國艾可蕾托 伊斯頓時，他的父親Sir William Bourchier47歲，母親Anne of Gloucester23歲。他於1426年4月25日之前與Isabel of Cambridge結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1483年4月4日在英國英格蘭艾可蕾托 伊斯頓享年79歲，埋葬在英國艾可馬爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex于大约1405年出生在英国艾可蕾托 伊斯頓时，他的父亲Sir William Bourchier47岁，母亲Anne of Gloucester23岁。他于1426年4月25日之前与Isabel of Cambridge结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1483年4月4日在英国英格兰艾可蕾托 伊斯頓享年79岁，埋葬在英国艾可馬爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Bourchier,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German   Buser  .
English (Kent): variant of   Bowser   1.

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: North German (also Börger) and Dutch: variant of Burger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from Yiddish borger ‘borrower’ or ‘lender’.
Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name for someone from a place called Borg (see   Borg  ), or a topographic name for someone living by a fortification or near a hill resembling a fortification.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex was born about 1405, in Little Easton, Essex, England, his father, Sir William Bourchier, was 47 and his mother, Anne of Gloucester, was 23. He married Isabel of Cambridge before 25 April 1426. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 4 April 1483, in Little Easton, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79, and was buried in Maldon, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex est né environ 1405, à Little Easton, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Bourchier, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Anne of Gloucester, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Isabel of Cambridge avant 25 avril 1426. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 4 avril 1483, à Little Easton, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterré à Maldon, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex um 1405 in Little Easton, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Bourchier 47 und seine Mutter Anne of Gloucester 23. Er hat vor 25. April 1426 Isabel of Cambridge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 4. April 1483 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Little Easton, Essex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Maldon, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex è nato il circa 1405 nel Little Easton, Essex, England, il suo padre, Sir William Bourchier, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Anne of Gloucester, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Isabel of Cambridge prima del 25 aprile 1426. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 4 aprile 1483, nel Little Easton, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni, e fu sepolto a Maldon, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essexが約1405年にイギリスエセックスリットル エアストンで生まれた時，父Sir William Bourchierは47歳，母Anne of Gloucesterは23歳であった。1426年4月25日以前にIsabel of Cambridgeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1483年4月4日にイギリスイギリスエセックスリットル エアストンで79歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスマルドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex(이)가 대략 1405년 영국 에세스 리틀 이스트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Bourchier(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne of Gloucester(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1426년 4월 25일 이전 Isabel of Cambridge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1483년 4월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀 이스트에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 맬던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex nasceu aproximadamente 1405, em Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Bourchier, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Anne of Gloucester, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel of Cambridge antes 25 de abril de 1426. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de abril de 1483, em Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 79 anos, e foi sepultado em Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex родился около 1405 г. в местности литл эастон, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Bourchier было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne of Gloucester, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel of Cambridge перед 25 апрель 1426 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 4 апреля 1483 г. в местности литл эастон, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет и был похоронен в местности малдон, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry Bourchier, Earl of Essex nació aproximadamente 1405, en Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Bourchier, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Anne of Gloucester, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Isabel of Cambridge antes de 25 de abril de 1426. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 4 de abril de 1483, en Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultado en Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra.
